Full job description
JOB TITLE: Operations Manager

REPORTS TO: Owner

Role Purpose

To represent the HR Hospitality brand and adopt a first-class approach across the guest base to maximise sales, revenue and operational standards. To provide effective leadership, management and budgetary control of the business and hospitality operation, and be accountable for achieving KPIs and objectives as agreed with the Owner. To maintain and develop strong guest relations at operational level at all times and identify opportunities for business growth.

Areas of Responsibility & Key Accountabilities

1. Driving Performance

Accountable for the achievement of specific KPIs agreed with your line manager in line with company strategy.
Key operational lead on all strategic initiatives, maintenance, labour scheduling, margin management, operating standards and compliance measures.
Interpret and analyse monthly management accounts and P&Ls. Benchmark performance and implement corrective measures and best practice.
Deliver projects on time, within budget, and fully embedded with designated owners.
Fully support and drive annual operational and marketing activities in line with set KPIs to increase turnover and profit.
Manage your team in line with agreed company processes and communicate effectively across all departments.
2. Guest & Competition

Ensure effective and timely communication of information to the General Manager, Head Chef and support teams.
Consistently carry out full venue walk rounds, highlighting areas requiring improvement from a team, operational and guest perspective.
Learn from internal and external guest feedback and ensure actions are implemented where appropriate.
Design and support the sharing of best practices and operational synergies.
3. Lead, Drive and Inspire

Lead, motivate and develop the General Manager and management teams to live company values, achieve objectives and deliver personal development goals.
Responsible for the day-to-day management of the General Manager(s) and/or management teams to maximise efficiency, operational standards and service levels – covering people, tasks, standards and behaviours.
Improve team quality and retention through effective recruitment, training, development and coaching.
Contribute to and support the implementation of the business plan in line with strategy and agreed KPIs.
Actively engage support teams to work in partnership to drive excellence across all areas.
Identify and support training needs and develop plans accordingly.
4. Processes & Compliance

Act as a brand custodian and ensure brand integrity is protected at all times through adherence to standards across Operations, HR, Marketing and Maintenance.
Support the execution of all compliance measures across the business.
Drive implementation of company policies and procedures.
Analyse available data to identify improvements and drive a culture of continuous improvement.
Role Dimensions

Financial (budgetary control etc.)

Non-financial (people management etc.)

Delivery of agreed targets/measures and project timelines

Direct reports Via scheduled performance reviews in line with job specifications

Typical Outputs (reporting requirements etc.)

Delivery of business plans
Producing statistical information regarding guest services as required
Communication, implementation and execution of the marketing calendar
Providing operational process information as required
Financial budget cycle reporting
Person Specification

Competency Requirements

Commercial and Financial AcumenApplies logical, well-developed ideas and solutions to issues. Considers the wider picture and value/cost benefits when making decisions.

Planning & Monitoring
Uses systems in place to plan and monitor work. Plans and monitors direct reports' work ensuring proactive support and guidance is provided.

Right First Time Delivery, Drive and OwnershipEnjoys responsibility and delivers against agreed targets. Works with pace, focus and optimism to overcome obstacles and deliver commercial solutions. Encourages others to remain positive.

Influencing with Impact
Presents strong arguments with passion, conviction and commercial credibility. Has the ability to prioritise and manage team and guest expectations.

Leadership
Actively encourages initiative, open communication and team engagement. Delegates appropriately and provides development opportunities. Provides constructive feedback, coaches others and addresses poor performance.

Team Player
Demonstrates company values, engages team members, treats everyone fairly, explains rather than directs, celebrates success and supports others.

Integrated Working
Builds effective working relationships across the business to ensure targets are delivered.

Technical / Professional Expertise Required

Previous experience working in a fast-paced, changeable environment.
Competent user of IT systems including Excel, Word and email systems.
Proven track record in a comparable role.
Ability to lead, mentor and coach others.
Excellent communication skills.
Strong relationship building skills.
Strong business and financial acumen.
Excellent attention to detail.
Self-motivated, ambitious and proactive self-starter.
Pay: £45,000.00-£51,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Company pension
Discounted or free food
Employee discount
Health & wellbeing programme
On-site parking
Private medical insurance
Work Location: In person

personalisedIntro: I bring a practical operations mindset and I’m at my best where day-to-day delivery, financial control and team leadership all need to work together. This role fits the kind of environment I’ve worked in before: fast-moving, accountable and dependent on clear standards, good judgement and follow-through.
whyThisRole: The work itself is the part that interests me most: holding performance, people, compliance and guest standards together in one role. Based on the brief, I’d be contributing where operational control, commercial awareness and consistent execution matter most, rather than just managing the day-to-day in isolation.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on margin, reporting and the practical levers that affect profit, so decisions are based on what the numbers are really saying.

focusAreasToBring.title: Standards and compliance

focusAreasToBring.title: People and retention
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on coaching, clarity and fair management so the team has the support it needs to perform well and stay engaged.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I’m a strong fit because my background combines operations, reporting, compliance and team leadership rather than treating them as separate tasks. I’ve worked in environments where standards matter, data needs to be acted on, and the operation has to keep moving while people are being led well.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months getting close to the operation, understanding the numbers, and making sure the key standards are being delivered consistently. I’d look to strengthen visibility around performance, support the management team, and turn guest feedback and compliance checks into practical actions that stick.
companyHighlights: Named hotel employer with a clear hospitality brand focus.
In-person role with benefits including pension, private medical insurance, wellbeing support and on-site parking.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people are expected to communicate openly, take ownership and treat standards seriously. The emphasis on team engagement, fair treatment and celebrating success fits the way I like to lead.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would spend time with the Owner, General Manager, Head Chef and support teams, learn the current KPIs, review reporting and get a feel for where the pressure points are in service, standards and people management.
I would focus on the main performance measures, compliance routines, labour scheduling and guest feedback actions, making sure issues are tracked clearly and ownership is understood.

I would start pushing the agreed actions into routine practice, support the management team with coaching and accountability, and help strengthen the link between performance, standards and business targets.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring calm, practical leadership and a clear focus on the numbers, the team and the guest experience.
closingProofPoints: I’ve helped improve financial discipline and cash performance.
I’ve built a practical compliance-tracking system in a regulated environment.
I’ve led teams through complex work while keeping service standards in place.
contentNotes: I have kept company-specific claims limited because the advert gives only basic company information.
I’ve selected evidence that supports commercial control, compliance and people leadership, as those appear to be the strongest needs in the brief.
No claims have been made about hotel-specific systems, size, or structure beyond what the advert states.
